meta.setting: Brush-choked roads, fire lines, pine slash, and chaparral edges around French Gulch and Clear Creek
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with labor, weather, animal pressure, road access, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Wintu fire stewardship context and the need to avoid inventing sacred fire practice.

research_grounding.title: Karuk Tribe
research_grounding.use: Cultural burning and environmental stewardship context for careful, respectful treatment.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Black bear habitat, food behavior, and sign in brush and forest edges.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Brush Crews cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented axe note, smoke warning, or line-camp saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Choppers and Burn-Line Hands
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing workers who cut brush, open roads, manage burn lines, and find what the hills were hiding
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate brush crews around hard labor, road clearing, smoke reading, animal flush, fire-line risk, hidden evidence, and local knowledge.
Acknowledge Native burning knowledge respectfully without assigning it as secret power or fake ceremony.
Tie future moves to cutting line, choosing when not to burn, bear sign, lost tools, found camps, blocked roads, smoke misread, and blister-cost travel.
Make the work hot, splintery, practical, and socially useful.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make every move leave splinters, sweat, and a choice about whether to let fire do the cleaner work. Brush is where the county hides until somebody puts an axe to it.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A brush-crew scene should ask what the crew uncovers, what the smoke means, and who benefits if the old road stays closed.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western labor and fire-line language only.
Keep fire stewardship respectful, grounded, and not invented as ceremony.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/brush-crews.png.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred-invented, or fake-tribal framing
do not make fire harmless, pretty, or purely symbolic
do not use modern slang, chainsaws, vehicles, modern fire gear, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about brush labor, fire-line judgment, animal sign, road access, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
